# MCP Custom Conditions Test
# This test demonstrates how to use custom conditions for implementation-specific features

# Protocol extension tests
[protocol_extension] echo "Protocol extension is enabled"
[!protocol_extension] echo "Protocol extension is not enabled"

# Custom feature tests
[custom_feature oauth] echo "OAuth custom feature is enabled"
[custom_feature saml] echo "SAML custom feature is enabled"
[custom_feature advanced_ui] echo "Advanced UI custom feature is enabled"

# Auth method tests
[auth_method basic] echo "Basic authentication is supported"
[auth_method saml] echo "SAML authentication is supported"
[auth_method oauth] echo "OAuth authentication is supported"

# Custom transport tests
[custom_transport grpc] echo "gRPC transport is supported"
[custom_transport quic] echo "QUIC transport is supported"
[custom_transport websocket] echo "WebSocket transport is supported"

# Runtime mode tests
[runtime_mode development] echo "Running in development mode"
[runtime_mode production] echo "Running in production mode"
[runtime_mode testing] echo "Running in testing mode"

# Version tests with custom features
[version 2025-03-26] [custom_feature oauth] echo "Using OAuth with 2025-03-26 protocol version"

# Combining standard and custom conditions
[http] [custom_transport grpc] echo "Both HTTP and gRPC transports are supported"
[stdio] [!custom_transport grpc] echo "stdio is supported but gRPC is not"

# Combining multiple custom conditions
[protocol_extension] [runtime_mode development] echo "Protocol extension in development mode"
[auth_method saml] [custom_feature oauth] echo "Both SAML and OAuth are supported"

# Complex conditions
[protocol_extension] [custom_feature oauth] [runtime_mode development] echo "All three conditions are satisfied"

# Testing tools with custom features
[tools] [custom_feature advanced_ui] exec mcp-send --custom-feature-flag

# Testing environment-specific setup
[env CI true] [runtime_mode testing] echo "Running in CI with testing mode"
